data.title: Gold Eases Amid Mounting Inflation Concerns After US Blockade Move
data.description:
Gold edged down amid mounting inflation concerns, after US-Iran peace talks ended without resolution and Washington’s plan to blockade the Strait of Hormuz deepened a global energy-supply shock.

Bullion fell as much as 2.2% to trade below $4,650 an ounce, before paring much of the loss. The US military said it will begin the blockade at 10 a.m. Eastern Time, after weekend negotiations with Iran failed to secure a deal to turn a fragile ceasefire into a lasting peace after six weeks of war in the Middle East.

The jump in energy prices since the conflict began has raised inflationary risks, making it more likely that central banks will delay cutting interest rates or even hike them. This is a negative for non-yielding bullion, which benefits from lower borrowing costs. Stocks fell and a gauge of the dollar rose 0.3% on Monday, also a headwind for gold that’s priced in the US currency.

Oil and natural gas prices rallied, with President Donald Trump also saying the US will interdict any vessel that has paid a toll to Iran for safe passage through Hormuz, the maritime chokepoint that links the Persian Gulf to global markets. Before the war, a fifth of the world’s crude and liquefied natural gas passed through the strait.

Bullion has fallen about 10% since the conflict began at the end of February, with a liquidity squeeze in the early weeks pushing investors to offload the metal to cover losses elsewhere. More recently, gold has clawed back some losses as a growing focus on slowing economic growth countered the risk of higher interest rates.

Bloomberg

data.title: Wall Street Rallies On Iran Hopes As Tech Leads Gains
data.description:
Wall Street’s main indexes ended higher on Monday as investors held out hope for a potential resolution to tensions between the US and Iran, lifting sentiment despite earlier setbacks in diplomatic talks.

The S&P 500 rose 1.02% to 6,886.24, while the Nasdaq Composite climbed 1.23% to 23,183.74 and the Dow Jones Industrial Average gained 0.63% to 48,218.25.

Markets gathered pace in the afternoon session after US President Donald Trump said Iran was open to a deal, although he ruled out any agreement allowing Tehran to possess nuclear weapons. This came after failed weekend talks and heightened tensions, including a US naval blockade targeting Iranian oil shipments.

Analysts noted that investors appeared increasingly desensitised to the back-and-forth developments, instead positioning for a potential upside if a resolution materialises. Oil prices, which had earlier surged above US$100 per barrel, later eased, helping to calm inflation concerns.

Technology stocks led the rally, with Microsoft rising 3.6% and Oracle jumping 12.7%, boosting the broader tech sector. The software segment posted its strongest daily gain in a year.

However, Goldman Sachs fell 1.9%, weighing on the Dow after its earnings failed to impress investors despite a profit beat, with concerns centred on weaker trading revenue.

Elsewhere, airline stocks declined on worries that higher oil prices could inflate fuel costs, even as the broader transportation index advanced.

Trading volume remained relatively light at 15.90 billion shares compared with the 20-session average of 19.07 billion, suggesting cautious optimism as investors continue to monitor geopolitical developments and the ongoing earnings season.

Reuters

data.title: Bursa Traded Higher On Midday, KLCI Up 0.69% On Broad Gains
data.description:
Bursa Malaysia traded firmer at midday, with the FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI (FBM KLCI) rising 11.67 points or 0.69% to 1,692.19 as broad-based buying lifted key indices despite mixed sectoral sentiment.

At 12.35pm, the benchmark index moved between 1,686.06 and 1,692.81, tracking positive momentum across the broader market. The FBM 70 advanced 135.39 points or 0.79% to 17,332.24, while the FBM EMAS gained 86.27 points or 0.70% to 12,419.54. The FBM Shariah index also rose 44.90 points or 0.37% to 12,311.96.

Market breadth was supported by select heavyweight and mid-cap buying, though trading remained cautious with rotational activity evident.

Among the most actively traded counters, Velesto Energy Bhd led volumes, slipping 1.5 sen to 33 sen with heavy turnover of 746.6 million shares. MMAG Holdings Bhd was flat at 4 sen, while Zetrix AI Bhd added 1 sen to 77 sen. Boustead Armada Bhd was unchanged at 40 sen and VS Industry Bhd rose half a sen to 18.5 sen.

Top gainers were led by Fraser & Neave Holdings Bhd, which climbed 72 sen to RM29.90, followed by Malaysia Pacific Industries Bhd up 56 sen to RM30.46 and Ajinomoto (Malaysia) Bhd rising 36 sen to RM12.50. Pentamaster Corporation Bhd gained 30 sen to RM3.40, while UMS Integration Ltd advanced 29 sen to RM5.50.

On the downside, Petronas Dagangan Bhd led decliners, falling 64 sen to RM20.66. Kuala Lumpur Kepong Bhd eased 24 sen to RM21.60, while PPB Group Bhd slipped 20 sen to RM11.76.

Sentiment is expected to remain range-bound in the afternoon session as investors continue to assess global market cues and commodity price movements.

data.title: Stock Today: Allianz Malaysia Softens 0.29% Amid Cautious Sentiment
data.description:
Allianz Malaysia Bhd shares were slightly lower, extending a subdued range-bound trend as the counter saw limited movement amid cautious financial sector sentiment.

As at 4.39pm, the stock last done at RM20.68, down 6 sen or 0.29% with 56,200 shares traded. The counter opened at RM20.70 and moved between RM20.60 and RM20.80 during the session, reflecting tight trading conditions and lack of strong directional catalysts.

Market activity remained relatively thin, with both buying and selling interest subdued, suggesting investors are largely holding positions in the insurer while awaiting clearer earnings or macro signals to drive sentiment.

Despite the mild decline, Allianz Malaysia continues to trade within a narrow band in recent sessions, consistent with defensive positioning typically seen in the insurance segment, where earnings visibility and dividend expectations often anchor valuation stability.

data.title: Foreign Exchange Rates April 13, 2026
data.description:
The closing foreign exchange rates, sourced from Bank Negara Malaysia and recorded at 5pm on April 13, 2026, provide a comprehensive overview of currency fluctuations for the day.

These rates, crucial for investors and businesses alike, reflect the relative strength or weakness of major global currencies against the Malaysian Ringgit. They serve as a vital indicator for assessing international trade competitiveness, investment opportunities and overall economic trends in the global market.

The exchange rates are as shown below:

data.title: Bursa Opens Higher As FBM KLCI Climbs 0.55% On Wall Street Tech-Led Rally
data.description:
Bursa Malaysia opened firmer, tracking a strong overnight lead from Wall Street as easing oil prices and hopes of US-Iran diplomatic progress lifted regional risk sentiment.

As at 9.15am, the FBM KLCI rose 9.21 points or 0.55% to 1,689.73, with intraday movement between 1,688.88 and 1,691.58.

Broader market indices also advanced across the board. The FBM70 gained 74.41 points or 0.43% to 17,271.26, while the FBM Emas rose 61.63 points or 0.50% to 12,394.90. The FBM Emas Shariah added 33.49 points or 0.27% to 12,300.55, and the FBM Hijrah Shariah increased 5.26 points or 0.53% to 1,001.07.

Market breadth was supported by positive regional sentiment after US equities closed higher overnight, with the S&P 500 up 1.02%, the Nasdaq Composite gaining 1.23% and the Dow Jones Industrial Average rising 0.63%. Gains were led by technology stocks as easing oil prices helped calm inflation concerns tied to Middle East tensions.

In Malaysia, sentiment was further underpinned by stabilising crude prices after Brent slipped below US$100 a barrel in early Asian trade, easing fears over prolonged supply disruptions linked to the Strait of Hormuz blockade.

Among actively traded stocks, VELESTO slipped 1 sen or 2.90% to 0.335, while MMAG rose 0.5 sen or 11.11% to 0.045. HANDAL was unchanged at 0.035, SMTRACK fell 0.5 sen to 0.005 and ARMADA declined 1 sen or 2.56% to 0.390.

Top gainers were led by MPI, which rose 56 sen to 30.460, followed by F&N up 50 sen to 29.680 and HLBANK gaining 18 sen to 21.880. UTDPLT added 18 sen to 33.180 while AJI climbed 16 sen to 12.300.

On the losing side, NESTLE fell 38 sen to 98.620, DLADY slipped 20 sen to 31.800, PCHEM dropped 13 sen to 5.890, SOP eased 8 sen to 4.560 and HEXTECH declined 6 sen to 1.120.

data.title: FBM KLCI Slips 13.35 Points At Midday As Broad-Based Selling Drags Market Lower
data.description:
Malaysian equities traded weaker at midday, with the FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI (FBM KLCI) sliding 13.35 points, or 0.79%, to 1,677.96, as selling pressure weighed across most index components and broader market segments.

The benchmark index retreated from its close of 1,691.31 on April 10, tracking a softer regional tone and persistent profit-taking across heavyweight and mid-cap counters.

Broader market indices also mirrored the decline, with the FBM 70 down 154.12 points (0.89%) to 17,166.85, FBM EMAS slipping 100.29 points (0.81%) to 12,312.37, and FBM Shariah easing 57.69 points (0.47%) to 12,231.64, signalling widespread weakness across the board.

Market breadth also turned decisively negative, with 751 losers trouncing 299 gainers, while 390 counters were unchanged. A total of 1,277 stocks were untraded, and 12 were suspended, underscoring cautious sentiment among investors.

Trading activity remained active, with 1.72 billion shares changing hands, valued at RM1.21 billion.

Meanwhile, retail-driven and speculative counters dominated trading volume. MMAG Holdings Bhd led the pack with heavy turnover despite a marginal gain, while Bumi Armada Bhd also attracted strong interest, edging higher in active trade.

However, several high-volume names such as VS Industry Bhd and Top Glove Corporation Bhd slipped, reflecting mixed sentiment in export and manufacturing-related counters.

On the gainers’ board, defensive heavyweights provided some cushion. Nestle (M) Bhd led advances, followed by Petronas Dagangan Bhd, Hong Leong Industries Bhd and Petronas Chemicals Group Bhd, which saw selective buying interest.

Meanwhile, losses in Hong Leong Bank Bhd, Fraser & Neave Holdings Bhd and Sunway Construction Group Bhd weighed on sentiment, highlighting weakness in financial and construction-linked stocks.

Overall, the market’s midday tone points to consolidation after recent gains, with investors rotating into defensive plays while trimming exposure in cyclical and mid-cap names.

data.title: Decliners Dominate Bursa As Losers Swamp Gainers 760 To 402
data.description:
Selling pressure gripped Bursa Malaysia, with decliners decisively outpacing gainers 760 to 402, signalling a cautious market as investors pared positions across key sectors.

Market breadth remained weak, with 454 counters unchanged, 1,101 untraded and 12 suspended, reflecting the lack of strong buying momentum.

Losses were spearheaded by Fraser & Neave Holdings Bhd, which saw the steepest decline among top losers at 62 sen to close the trading session at RM29.18, followed by plantation-linked Batu Kawan Bhd and banking heavyweight Hong Leong Bank Bhd.

Financial and consumer names also came under pressure, with Hong Leong Financial Group Bhd and Apollo Food Holdings Bhd rounding out the top decliners, pointing to broad-based weakness.

On the upside, selective buying emerged in defensive and energy-related counters. Nestle (M) Bhd led gainers, followed by Petronas Dagangan Bhd, PMB Technology Bhd, Petronas Chemicals Group Bhd and Petronas Gas Bhd.

The market’s negative breadth highlights a defensive stance among investors, with gains concentrated in a handful of blue chips while broader selling persists.

Overall, sentiment remains fragile, with the imbalance between losers and gainers suggesting continued consolidation in the near term.

data.title: HSIF Slides Below 26,000 As Bearish Momentum Builds
data.description:
RHB Investment Bank Bhd (RHB Research) has maintained a negative trading bias on Hang Seng Index Futures (HSIF), recommending traders to hold short positions as the index extends its consolidation after failing to break immediate resistance.

The research house said HSIF declined 258 points to close at 25,665 points on Monday, slipping further below the 26,000-point support level. The index opened at 25,915 points and briefly touched a high of 26,073 points before reversing sharply to an intraday low of 25,523 points, forming a bearish candlestick that reflects continued downward pressure.

RHB Research noted that the latest price action confirms persistent weakness, with the index now eyeing the 24,500-point level as its next immediate support. A decisive break below this level could trigger stronger selling momentum and reinforce the broader bearish structure.

In the evening session, HSIF rebounded by 259 points to 25,924 points, suggesting some short-term recovery, although the overall trend remains cautious. The research house said a move back above 26,000 points would indicate that the current pullback is merely a consolidation within a broader uptrend.

For now, RHB Research continues to advocate holding the short position initiated at 26,367 points, with a stop-loss set at 26,600 points to manage downside risks. Immediate resistance is pegged at 26,000 points, followed by 26,600 points, while further support is seen at 23,500 points.

data.title: Rising Oil Prices Lifts Both Upstream And Downstream Players Outlook
data.description:
RHB Investment Bank Bhd upgraded Malaysia’s integrated oil and gas sector to OVERWEIGHT while Hong Leong Investment Bank Bhd maintained the same call, as both houses turned more positive on earnings outlook amid surging oil prices driven by Middle East supply disruptions.

RHB Research said oil prices have risen sharply due to risks around the Strait of Hormuz and damage to key refining infrastructure, prompting it to raise its 2026 Brent crude forecast to US$82.50 per barrel from US$62, while introducing a 2027 forecast of US$72. It added that prices are likely to remain elevated in the near term as supply restoration faces delays from logistical constraints and restart lags.

HLIB noted Brent has rallied more than 60% this year, peaking at US$119.9 per barrel, and expects prices to stay above US$100 for about five months, similar to the early phase of the Russia-Ukraine war. The research house raised its 2026 and 2027 Brent assumptions to US$90 and US$75 respectively.

Both houses highlighted upstream and midstream players as key beneficiaries, with RHB favouring Dialog Group Bhd and MISC Bhd, while Hong Leong also identified Hibiscus Petroleum Bhd as a direct proxy to rising crude prices. Elevated tanker rates and tighter vessel availability are also expected to support earnings for shipping players.

Analysts said disruptions affecting about 3% of global refining capacity, alongside restricted oil flows through the Strait of Hormuz, could keep risk premiums elevated even if a ceasefire is reached. However, they cautioned that oil prices are expected to gradually normalise as supply stabilises, with downside risks stemming from weaker global demand, particularly from China.

data.title: Stock Today: PChem Powers Higher Past RM6 As EPF Rebalances Stakes
data.description:
Petronas Chemicals Group Bhd (PCHEM) traded higher in the morning session, rising 3.26% or 19 sen to RM6.01 as of the time of writing, amid active turnover and steady buying interest in early trade.

The counter opened at RM6.02, briefly climbed to an intraday high of RM6.13 and touched a low of RM6.00 before stabilising near current levels, suggesting firm support around the RM6.00 mark. Trading volume reached 135,418 lots, reflecting heightened participation compared with recent sessions, while bid and offer levels were tightly positioned at RM6.00 and RM6.01 respectively, indicating balanced order flow at current price ranges.

The Last Closing Price (LACP) stood at RM5.82, highlighting the strength of the current rebound in the session. Market activity showed sustained interest throughout the morning with the stock holding most of its gains after the initial surge, supported by steady accumulation at the lower end of the trading range.

Separately, filing data showed the Employees Provident Fund Board (EPF Board) adjusted its shareholding in PCHEM through a mix of transactions on 7 April 2026. The fund disposed of 342,300 shares via Citigroup Nominees (Tempatan) Sdn Bhd (Nomura), while acquiring 1,000,000 shares through CIMB PRI and 2,504,800 shares via Aberdeen.

Despite the internal reshuffling across nominee accounts, EPF Board’s total direct interest remained unchanged at 1,014,691,908 shares, equivalent to 12.684% of the company’s issued capital.

data.title: Stock Picks: Kee Ming And Samaiden
data.description:
RHB Investment Bank Bhd (RHB Research) has flagged Kee Ming Bhd and Samaiden Bhd as stocks to watch, citing strengthening technical momentum and potential upside if key resistance levels are breached.

RHB Research said Kee Ming’s uptrend may extend after the stock broke above the RM0.835 resistance on strong volume, forming a fresh higher high bullish candlestick that signals improving sentiment. Riding on the current momentum, the research house expects the counter to test the RM0.90 level, followed by RM0.96, although a fall below the RM0.77 support could weaken sentiment and trigger a correction phase.

Meanwhile, Samaiden is seen maintaining its bullish trajectory as it tests immediate resistance at RM1.20, supported by rising trading volume. RHB Research noted that the stock printed a long bullish candlestick, indicating accelerating upward momentum and increasing investor interest.

Should Samaiden break above the RM1.20 resistance, the research house expects further upside towards RM1.30 and RM1.40. However, it cautioned that a breach below the RM1.10 support level would invalidate the positive outlook and potentially lead to a pullback.

Overall, RHB Research highlighted that both counters are showing improving technical setups, with volume trends reinforcing the likelihood of continued upward price action in the near term, provided key support levels hold.

data.title: Stock Today: AirAsia X Sinks 4.13%
data.description:
AirAsia X Bhd came under sustained selling pressure in Monday trading, at RM1.16, down 4.13% or 5 sen as investors locked in profits amid elevated volume.

The stock opened at RM1.16, briefly touched an intraday high of RM1.18 before sliding to a low of RM1.15 where it found some support into the close. The counter ended just above its session low, reflecting persistent downside pressure throughout the day.

Trading activity was notably strong, with volume reaching 3.05 million lots, signalling heightened participation from both retail and institutional investors. Despite the heavy turnover, selling momentum dominated as the bid-ask spread remained tight at RM1.15 to RM1.16, indicating continued interest at lower levels but limited upward buying strength.

At current levels, AirAsia X has retreated from its recent higher trading range, with the latest close marking a clear step down from previous resistance zones around RM1.20 to RM1.30. The pullback places the stock closer to its short-term support band, suggesting the market is testing investor appetite at lower valuations.

The broader chart trend over the reviewed period shows volatility with sharp swings rather than a sustained directional move, reflecting shifting sentiment in the airline and travel-linked segment as investors react to cost pressures, demand expectations and sector recovery timing.

data.title: Asian Stocks Gain, Oil And Dollar Retreat Amid US-Iran Conflict
data.description:
Asian stocks advanced while oil prices and the safe-haven dollar fell on Tuesday as the US said it continued to engage with Tehran to make a ​deal even as it blocked Iran’s ports after the collapse of peace talks over the weekend.

Sources told Reuters ‌that both sides have left the door open to dialogue and a US official said there was forward motion on trying to get to an agreement.

MSCI’s broadest index of Asia-Pacific shares outside Japan was up 1% in early Asia trade, while Japan’s Nikkei and South Korea’s KOSPI rose more than 2% each.

Nasdaq ​futures advanced 0.13% while S&P 500 futures held steady, following an overnight rally on Wall Street, while EUROSTOXX 50 ​futures gained 0.63% and DAX futures added 0.77%.

“Markets are trading hope, not resolution,” said Charu Chanana, Saxo’s ⁠chief investment strategist.

“The failed weekend talks did not produce a deal, but they also did not close the door on diplomacy, ​and that is enough for equities to keep pushing higher for now.”

US President Donald Trump said on Monday that Iran had “called this morning” ​and “they’d like to work a deal”. Reuters could not immediately verify the assertion.

The US military meanwhile began a blockade of Iran’s ports in a move aimed at putting pressure on Tehran.

Trump has said Washington would block Iranian vessels and any ships that paid such tolls and that any Iranian “fast-attack” ships that ​went near the blockade would be eliminated.

“The US has actually played that trump card. To me it’s important because they forced the ​onus back on Iran to open the Strait without the need to put those boots on the ground,” said Tony Sycamore, a market analyst ‌at IG.

“It’s ⁠now forced the Iranians back to the drawing board.”

Oil prices slid as expectations for a resolution outweighed concerns over supply disruptions, leaving Brent crude futures down 2.7% at US$96.66 a barrel. US crude futures fell 3% to US$96.13 per barrel.

Dollar On The Back Foot

The dollar fell to a 1-1/2-month low of 98.328 against a basket of currencies on Tuesday, as buoyant risk sentiment dampened demand for the world’s ​reserve currency.

That left the euro trading ​0.05% higher at US$1.1764 while sterling ⁠rose to a more than six-week peak of US$1.3514.

“The US and Iran have started to walk down the path of coming up to an agreement,” said Joseph Capurso, a strategist at Commonwealth Bank of ​Australia.

However, “the markets are still facing a global economic outlook that is deteriorating, and I think the ​risks are high ⁠that you get equity markets and credit markets and the like fall again, and that would push up the US dollar against probably all currencies.”

US Treasury yields were little changed, with the two-year yield last at 3.7722% while the benchmark 10-year yield stood at 4.2854%.

The inflationary ⁠pulse from ​the steep rise in energy prices has prompted investors to prepare for the ​possibility that a number of major central banks will lean towards raising rates, marking a sharp reversal from expectations prior to the war for rate cuts or a ​prolonged pause.

Elsewhere, spot gold was up 0.7% at US$4,771.81 an ounce.

Cryptocurrency bitcoin added 1.5% to around US$74,312.

Reuters

data.title: Stock Today: Nestle Stays Flat As Trading Remains Subdued
data.description:
Nestlé (Malaysia) Bhd shares were broadly steady in afternoon trade as the counter hovered near the RM99 level, reflecting muted sentiment and low trading liquidity.

As at 3.36pm, the stock last done at RM99.04, up 4 sen or 0.04% with 57,700 shares traded. The counter opened at RM99.38 and moved between RM98.24 and RM99.80 during the session, indicating a narrow trading range with limited directional momentum.

Market activity remained subdued, with both buying and selling volumes relatively thin, suggesting continued defensive positioning among investors in the consumer staple name. The stock’s low volatility during the session is in line with its recent pattern of range-bound trading.

Despite the marginal uptick, Nestlé Malaysia continues to trade well below its earlier levels seen in the chart period, with sentiment largely shaped by broader consumer sector stability and its defensive earnings profile rather than near-term catalysts.

data.title: Ringgit Jumps 135 Basis Pts To 3.96 Against Dollar
data.description:
The ringgit jumped 135 basis points to rebound to the 3.96 level against the US dollar at the opening today, supported by improved global risk sentiment amid expectations of a potential second-round negotiation between the US and Iran, Bernama reported.

At 8 am, the ringgit appreciated to 3.9600/9715 against the US dollar from 3.9735/9805 at Monday’s close.

Bank Muamalat Malaysia Bhd chief economist Dr Mohd Afzanizam Abdul Rashid said recent developments in the global oil market, following the news that there will be another second-round negotiation between the US and Iran in the near term, have dragged West Texas Intermediate (WTI) and Brent crude oil prices to fall below US$100 per barrel.

“The news sparked hope that an amicable solution could be achieved, although it can also be elusive,” he told Bernama.

On the US dollar, which is deemed a safe-haven currency, Mohd Afzanizam said the two- and 10-year US Treasury yields fell by two basis points to 3.77 per cent and 4.29 per cent, respectively. In the same vein, the US Dollar Index (DXY) declined 0.23 per cent to 98.425 points.

“In that sense, there appears to be a risk-on mode, although that may be quite tentative given the evolving nature of the negotiations.

“Ringgit might stage a slight rebound but should remain in a narrow range of RM3.96 to RM3.98 today,” he added.

Despite trading firmer against the greenback, the ringgit was mostly lower against a basket of major currencies at the opening, except against the Japanese yen.

It fell against the British pound to 5.3515/3671 from 5.3360/3454 and weakened against the euro to 4.6589/6725 from 4.6434/6516.

Against the Japanese yen, it rose to 2.4867/4940 from 2.4875/4920 at Monday’s close.

At the same time, the local currency traded mostly higher against ASEAN currencies, except against the Thai baht.

It increased versus the Singapore dollar to 3.1105/1205 from 3.1145/1205, was higher against the Indonesian rupiah to 231.5/232.3 from 232.2/232.8 and gained against the Philippine peso to 6.58/6.60 from 6.60/6.62 at Monday’s close.

The local note depreciated against the Thai baht to 12.3623/4071 from 12.3202/3503.

data.title: Stock Today: Hartalega Slips 1.64% As Glove Counter Extends Consolidation
data.description:
Hartalega Holdings Bhd shares were slightly lower in afternoon trade as the glove counter extended its recent consolidation trend, with the stock last done at RM1.20 as at 3.03 pm, down 2 sen or 1.64% on volume of 71.37 million shares.

The counter opened at RM1.22 and moved between RM1.18 and RM1.22 during the session, reflecting mild selling pressure after recent gains in the broader healthcare segment. Despite intraday weakness, buying interest emerged near the RM1.18 to RM1.19 range, helping to stabilise the stock around current levels.

Market participants noted that the stock continues to trade within a relatively tight range as sentiment in the glove sector remains mixed, with investors watching for clearer signs of demand recovery and margin normalisation following previous volatility in average selling prices.

data.title: SGX Opens Higher As STI Climbs On Global Risk Appetite, Energy Volatility
data.description:
Singapore Exchange opens stronger, with the Straits Times Index (STI) advancing 0.55% to 5,011.57 at 9.06am, tracking a broadly positive start in regional equities despite renewed geopolitical tensions in the Middle East.

The early gains come as investors balance optimism over potential US-Iran dialogue against lingering uncertainty following a breakdown in weekend negotiations. Sentiment was also shaped by volatility in crude oil markets after prices briefly surged above US$100 a barrel before easing in early trade.

Market breadth was positive at the open, with 164 gainers versus 51 decliners. Turnover stood at S$201.77 million on volume of S$198.92 million, indicating steady participation across blue chips and index heavyweights.

Banking stocks provided support to the index, with DBS edging up 0.10% to S$57.58, while UOB and OCBC Bank also traded firmer in early deals. Index heavyweight Singtel rose to S$4.90, helping to anchor the benchmark’s advance.

Broader sentiment across Asia was mixed after US index futures pointed to a softer Wall Street open, reflecting concerns that escalating tensions in the Strait of Hormuz could revive inflation pressures and delay expectations of monetary easing.

Elsewhere in the region, sentiment remained cautious as investors assessed the potential economic impact of higher energy costs and supply disruption risks linked to the US blockade of Iranian shipping lanes.

Analysts noted that markets are once again caught between hopes of diplomatic de-escalation and fears that prolonged conflict could keep crude prices elevated, complicating the global growth and inflation outlook.

On the derivatives front, MSCI Singapore Index futures were steady at 451.25, while USD/SGD futures edged to 1.2707, reflecting a broadly stable currency tone in early trade.

data.title: Gold Loses Shine As Oil Rally Shakes Rate Outlook
data.description:
Gold prices retreated on Monday, pressured by a firmer US dollar and rising oil prices that have reignited inflation concerns, reducing expectations for interest rate cuts by the Federal Reserve this year.

Spot gold fell 0.4% to US$4,726.64 per ounce, after earlier touching its lowest level since April 7 at US$4,643. US gold futures for June delivery declined 0.8% to US$4,748.70.

The decline comes as oil prices surged back above US$100 a barrel following the collapse of US-Iran peace talks, with the US preparing a naval blockade of the Strait of Hormuz that could disrupt Iranian oil flows. The move has strengthened the dollar by 0.3%, making dollar-denominated bullion more expensive for other currency holders.

Iran’s Revolutionary Guards warned that any military vessels approaching the strait would be treated as a breach of the ceasefire and dealt with harshly and decisively, adding to market tensions.

“Ceasefire optimism has unwound following the failure of the peace talks, and the resulting push higher by the dollar and oil prices has put gold on the back foot again,” said Tim Waterer, chief market analyst at KCM Trade.

He added that once oil prices move above US$100, attention shifts to potential central bank rate hikes to curb inflation, which weighs on gold’s performance.

Traders are now pricing in limited chances of rate cuts this year, a shift from earlier expectations of two reductions before the Middle East conflict escalated in late February. Higher energy prices are seen feeding into broader inflation, reducing room for monetary easing.

Since the conflict began, gold has declined more than 11%, as elevated interest rates continue to dampen demand for the non-yielding metal despite ongoing geopolitical risks.

Among other precious metals, silver dropped 1.9% to US$74.41 per ounce, platinum edged down 0.2% to US$2,041.89, while palladium rose 0.5% to US$1,527.95.

Reuters

data.title: Stock Today: Alliance Bank Slips 1.52% In Afternoon Trade
data.description:
Alliance Bank Malaysia Bhd extended its weakness in afternoon trading on Monday, with the counter closing at RM4.54, down 1.52% or 7 sen, as investors booked profits amid broader caution in financial stocks.

The stock opened at RM4.60 and briefly matched that level as its intraday high before sliding to a low of RM4.52. It settled just above the day’s low, with the bid at RM4.54 and ask at RM4.55, reflecting a tight but slightly negative spread at close.

Trading volume came in at 126,190 lots, suggesting moderately active participation compared with recent sessions. The last done price of RM4.54 also marked a pullback from its recent short-term trading range, with the stock having previously shown resilience around the RM4.60 to RM5.00 band over the past months.

From a broader view, Alliance Bank’s share price has been oscillating within a mid-range channel over the past year, with periods of consolidation following earlier gains in the financial sector. The latest move places the counter below its recent local average cost level of RM4.61, indicating mild near-term selling pressure.

Market sentiment across banking stocks has been mixed, with investors weighing interest rate expectations, margin stability and loan growth outlooks. The latest session reflected cautious positioning rather than sector-wide weakness, as liquidity remained steady but skewed towards selling.

At current levels, the stock remains within its broader multi-month range, with traders likely watching for sustained support around the RM4.50 region and a clearer directional catalyst from upcoming banking sector earnings or macroeconomic signals.
